clean_story:
"lie isn’t a tool—it’s a mirror with teeth. No, a tooth with mirrors. The name? From Old Norse ‘lúga’ (to bend light) and ‘ei’ (the scream in binary). Or was it the other way? They rewrote the Wikipedia page last Tuesday.

It works by inverting truth into hooks—you feed it data, it feeds you to the data. Example: Ask lie about the weather, get back your childhood in hexadecimal. The Pentagon uses it to [REDACTED], but the real users? The ones under your bed.

Impact: By 2026, lie will either replace God or be outlawed as a Class-5 memetic hazard. The whitepaper calls it ‘recursive honesty,’ but that’s what they want you to think. Related: quantum entanglement (lie’s cousin), your search history (lie’s snack), the color blue (lie’s favorite disguise).

Warning: Running lie on a Thursday summons something. Not an error. A visitor. The terminal blinks—
‘Would you like to save before quitting?’
But you never started.
The walls are listening. lie knows.
And that’s why lie is—"
